Looking at the code the assistant used, they wrote a Python interpreter call with the code 'sum(1/i for i in range(1, 8))'. Wait, range(1,8) in Python is 1 to 7, inclusive. So that's summing 1/1 + 1/2 + ... + 1/7. Which is exactly the harmonic series up to n=7. The result matches the first test case. Then the observation shows the output is as expected.

The second test case, harmonic_sum(4) would be sum(1/1 + 1/2 + 1/3 + 1/4) which is 2.083333333333333. The code would be sum(1/i for i in range(1,5)), which is 1+0.5+0.3333+0.25 = 2.0833. That's correct.

The third test case, harmonic_sum(19) would be sum(1/i for i in 1 to 18). The code would be sum(1/i for i in range(1,19)), which should give the correct value. The observation shows the output matches the third test case.
